phase1.title: Audit all interactive elements for data-testid coverage
Add data-testid to TopNavBar, QuantitiesPanel, ScalePanel, ClassificationGroups
Verify SF labels always visible on every polygon type
Canvas must accept standard PointerEvents for drawing
Document canvas coordinate contract for agent programming
CDP interaction test suite — verify all draw tools

phase2.title: Auto-naming sheets from title blocks on upload
Pattern Search — find repeated symbols across pages
Drawing Comparison — compare revisions, quantify changes
Merge tool — fill gaps between polygons
Split tool — divide polygon in two
Cut/subtract tool — remove area from polygon
Door/window backout from wall linear measurements

phase3.title: Assemblies — link classifications to materials + costs
Custom formulas (Excel-like syntax)
phase3.notes: CustomFormulas.tsx existed but was dead code — re-dispatched A5 to wire it in

bugs.description: save-to-org-btn used raw 'mx-org-library' localStorage key; ClassificationLibrary reads from 'mx-org-classifications'. Saved items never appeared in the org library panel.
Assembly cost estimate cast as { unitCost?: number } but Assembly type has no unitCost field — always produced $0.00. Fixed to compute cost from materials[].unitCost * (1/coverageRate).

cursor:update SSE event fell into default case — not routed to activity bus. useCollaboration hook never received cursor positions from other users. Fixed by adding cursor:update to the SSE event switch.

Collaborator cursors read via useStore.getState() (non-reactive) — cursors would never update in the render without a forced re-render trigger. Fixed by adding reactive useStore selector for collaborators at component top-level.

CustomFormulas component (P3-03) was built but never imported or wired into QuantitiesPanel. Users had no way to access custom formula editor. Fixed by: adding import, formulaModalClassification state, edit-row button with preview, modal render in all layouts, formula field preservation in saveEditing.

ClassificationUpdateSchema only included name/type/color/visible. All extended fields (formula, formulaUnit, formulaSavedToLibrary, tradeGroup, tileWidth, tileHeight, tileUnit, slopeFactor, breakdowns, customProperties, backouts, deductions) were silently stripped by Zod on every PATCH/PUT to /api/projects/[id]/classifications/[cid]. Custom formula saves via P3-03 modal were never persisted.

handleFullExport was client-side ExcelJS with only a flat quantities sheet — assemblies, estimates, and custom properties were never included in the download. Fixed by delegating to server-side /api/projects/[id]/export/excel route which generates all 5 sheets (Summary, Quantities, Assemblies, Estimates, Custom Properties).

deletePolygon ops in batch route called getPolygons(id) inside the loop — O(n) file reads for n deletePolygon ops (up to 500 ops = 500 file reads). Fixed by pre-loading all polygon IDs before the loop and using a Set for O(1) membership checks. Also update set on createPolygon/deletePolygon to keep consistent within same batch.

EstimatesTab built assemblyByType keyed by quantityFormula ("area"/"linear"/"count") — all classifications of the same type shared one assembly's unit cost, ignoring per-classification assembly assignments. Fixed by matching on classificationId first, falling back to formula/type match for unlinked classifications.

Custom formulas (P3-03) were stored in classification.formula but never applied to estimateQuantities. Formula result was displayed in the modal preview only — it never overrode the raw area/linear/count used in EstimatesTab cost calculations. Fixed by: (1) extracting tokenizer/evaluator to shared src/lib/formula-eval.ts, (2) applying applyCustomFormula() in estimateQuantities useMemo before falling back to raw geometry values.

AssembliesPanel used single project-level ppu for all pages — multi-page projects with per-page scales computed wrong quantities in Assemblies tab. Fixed to use scales[polygon.pageNumber] with fallback (mirrors QuantitiesPanel pattern).

GET and POST /api/estimates used single project-level ppu (getScale page-1 only) for all polygons across all pages. Multi-page projects computed wrong quantities server-side. Same root cause as BUG-PIKE-011 (AssembliesPanel). Fixed by importing listScales() and building per-page scale map with page-1 fallback — mirrors excel export route pattern.
/api/quantities used single page-1 ppu for all polygons — multi-page projects had incorrect quantity calculations on API/export side

Server-side quantities API and Excel export did not apply classification.formula overrides — client QuantitiesPanel showed formula results but API/export returned raw area/linear/count, creating data mismatch

CustomFormulas formula preview used single page-1 scale for all pages (multi-page inaccuracy) AND treated p.linearFeet as real-world units but it is raw pixels (linear_pixels in DB) — formula preview showed wildly inflated linear values. Fixed: use per-page scales from store.scales map and divide p.linearFeet by ppu.
AI takeoff summary used single global ppu for all pages + poly.linearFeet (raw pixels) instead of recalculating linear from points. Multi-page projects showed wrong LF totals.
